items.q: Confit is always a dry-heat grilling method.
items.explanation: This is a standard hospitality and kitchen principle.

items.q: Al dente pasta is intentionally overcooked until soft.
items.explanation: This is a standard hospitality and kitchen principle.

items.q: Which answer best fits this culinary concept: food allergen cross-contact means?
items.explanation: This concept maps to unintended transfer of allergen.

items.q: What culinary term or concept matches: term for cooking meat in its own fat? (set 2)
items.explanation: Hospitality training commonly defines this as confit.

items.q: What culinary term or concept matches: food allergen cross-contact means? (set 2)
items.explanation: Hospitality training commonly defines this as unintended transfer of allergen.

items.q: What culinary term or concept matches: thickener made from equal fat and flour? (set 2)
items.explanation: Hospitality training commonly defines this as roux.

items.q: What culinary term or concept matches: mother sauce made from clarified butter and egg yolk? (set 2)
items.explanation: Hospitality training commonly defines this as hollandaise.

items.q: What culinary term or concept matches: mother sauce based on tomatoes? (set 2)
items.explanation: Hospitality training commonly defines this as tomato sauce.

items.q: What culinary term or concept matches: protein network formed when kneading wheat dough? (set 2)
items.explanation: Hospitality training commonly defines this as gluten.

items.q: What culinary term or concept matches: mother sauce from brown stock and brown roux? (set 2)
items.explanation: Hospitality training commonly defines this as espagnole.
